steps.title: Count the novels and protagonists
steps.content: The passage explicitly names two novels—Northanger Abbey and The Mysteries of Udolpho—and two protagonists: Catherine Morland and Emily St. Aubert. The word “both” before Catherine confirms we're comparing two characters. Both novels and protagonists must be plural.

steps.title: Apply the plural possessive rule
steps.content: The experiences belong to the protagonists of the novels. Both nouns must be possessive: novels' and protagonists'. For plural nouns, the possessive form is apostrophe after the s. The phrase novels' protagonists' experiences means “the experiences of the protagonists of the novels.” The other choices either omit the possessive on protagonists (A) or use singular forms (C, D) that contradict the passage's reference to two novels and two protagonists. That matches choice B — B is correct.
